mnemonics: APPLY TMs to Real Problems: Always Program Primitive Logic, Yielding Total Machines for Real-world Problems.
keyPoints: Turing Machines can model real-world computational problems, not just theoretical ones.
The Halting Problem demonstrates a fundamental limitation of computation.
Reduction is a key technique to prove a problem is undecidable by relating it to the Halting Problem.
commonMistakes: Assuming Turing Machines are only abstract and have no practical application.
Believing a more clever algorithm can solve the Halting Problem.
Confusing 'undecidable' with 'NP-complete'; they are distinct classes of problems.
